reason: Cannot satisfy quality rubrics in this environment. Two hard blockers: (1) /workspace/task/tests/ is read-only (EACCES on Edit/Write), so test_outputs.py and tests/eval_manifest.yaml cannot be modified — this matches the prior tests_rewrite_status.json; (2) Bash tool fails with EACCES creating /home/worker/.claude/session-env, so docker build/run cannot be executed to validate nop/gold rewards. Made the following best-effort fixes within writable scope: (a) /workspace/task/eval_manifest.yaml updated so check ids match the actual test function names in test_outputs.py (test_column_prop_is_functional, test_useFilledColumns_hook_exists_and_runs, test_column_defaults_are_applied, test_hook_returns_original_when_no_defaults, test_special_columns_preserved, test_nested_column_groups_work, test_children_property_not_duplicated, test_useFilledColumns_integration_in_table, test_demo_file_exists, test_internal_tests_exist); (b) instruction.md tightened — removed the 'Reference' paragraph that described the gold patch shape, added explicit hook-surface contract (name, location, signature, identity-preservation when defaults falsy) and explicit Table.SELECTION_COLUMN / Table.EXPAND_COLUMN preservation requirement so every value the tests assert on is recoverable from the spec, while keeping the description symptom/contract-oriented rather than step-by-step. Note: current test_outputs.py is already substantially better than the snapshot quality.json was judged against — it uses subprocess.run + tsx to execute behavior (not grep), so tests_verify_behavior_not_text and test_not_tautological would likely re-judge differently. solution_uniqueness_guard remains a residual risk because tests import the exact path components/table/hooks/useFilledColumns; relaxing that requires editing tests, which is blocked. Prior validate node already recorded nop_reward=0.0, gold_reward=1.0 for this test file.
